subject: Question about my July water bill
body_clean:
Dear Mayor Okonkwo,

I hope you'll forgive me for writing directly, but I'm not sure where else to turn. My water bill this month came to nearly three times what I usually pay, and I live alone with no leaks I can find. I've kept every bill for years, and nothing on my end has changed. I suspect the meter was read wrong. Could someone in the water office please take a look at my account? I'd be very grateful. I keep careful records if that helps.

Thank you for your time.

subject: Your water account has been corrected
body_clean:
Dear Ms. Bennett,

Thank you for flagging this, and the Mayor's office asked me to follow up directly. I pulled your account and you were right. The June read came back as an estimate because the meter couldn't be accessed, and the system carried that forward as an unusually high figure. I sent a tech out for an actual read this morning. Your usage is right in line with your normal pattern, so I've reversed the overcharge and applied a credit of $128.40 to your next cycle. No action needed on your part. Apologies for the worry.

subject: Deep pothole on 25th Ave by my driveway
body_clean:
Dear Mayor Okonkwo,

There is a pothole on 25th Ave just south of my driveway that has gotten quite deep this fall. It's wide enough now that cars swing into the oncoming lane to avoid it, and I worry someone will damage a tire or worse. I measured it at about eight inches across and several inches deep. With the cold coming I imagine it will only get worse. Could Public Works have a look before the snow? Thank you, as always, for your attention.

subject: Thank you, sending Public Works out
body_clean:
Dear Ms. Bennett,

Thank you for the careful note, and for the measurements, which actually help our crews more than you'd think. I've forwarded this to Frank DiMeo in Public Works and asked them to get a patch on it before the weather turns. They'll do a temporary cold patch now and a proper fix when the asphalt plants reopen in spring. Please keep letting us know when you see things like this. Residents who pay attention to their block make our whole town safer. I'll follow up if I don't hear back from the crew.

subject: A thank you to you and your crews
body_clean:
Dear Mayor Okonkwo,

I wanted to write with good news for a change. The pothole on 25th Ave was patched, and it has held up beautifully through all this January snow. I also want to mention the plow driver who came through during the big storm. He took care not to bury the end of my driveway and even gave a wave. Small kindnesses, but they matter a great deal to those of us who shovel alone. Please pass my thanks along to him and to Mr. DiMeo. You run a responsive office, and I notice.

subject: Tree limb hiding the stop sign at my corner
body_clean:
Dear Mayor Okonkwo,

The parkway tree at my corner of 25th Ave has leafed out heavily this year, and one low limb has grown right across the stop sign. From a car approaching, you really can't see the sign until you're nearly on top of it. I watched two cars roll through yesterday afternoon, and I don't think it was carelessness so much as that they simply couldn't see it. It seems like a real hazard with the children out for summer. Could forestry trim it back? I'd hate for it to take an accident.

subject: Forestry will trim that limb
body_clean:
Hi Ms. Bennett,

Thanks for the heads-up, and the Mayor passed your note to me. You're right that a blocked stop sign is a safety issue we treat as a priority, so I've put it on our forestry list with a note about the sightline. Our crew has a run scheduled in your area and will get to it within the next two weeks, weather permitting. They'll clear the limb and give the whole canopy a look while they're up there so it doesn't creep back over the sign by next summer. I'll close the ticket once it's done.

subject: Leaf and yard waste pickup missed twice
body_clean:
Dear Mayor Okonkwo,

I don't like to be a bother, but our block has now been skipped twice for yard waste pickup. I set my paper bags out the night before both Tuesdays, as I always do, and both times the truck went by without stopping. Several neighbors are in the same boat, and the bags are piling up along the parkway looking rather sad. I know fall is your busiest stretch for this. Could someone check whether our street got dropped from the route? Thank you for looking into it.

subject: Streetlight out on 25th Ave
body_clean:
Dear Mayor Okonkwo,

The streetlight near my corner on 25th Ave has been out for about a week now. It may seem minor, but that stretch is quite dark in the early morning when I walk to the seven o'clock service, and the sidewalk is uneven there. I've caught myself once already. With the time change it's still dark at that hour. I'd feel much safer if it could be repaired. I believe the pole has a small number plate on it if that helps the crew locate the right one. Thank you for your help.

subject: Storm drain flooding the crosswalk at my corner
body_clean:
Dear Mayor Okonkwo,

I'm writing once more about my corner of 25th Ave, which I seem to do more than I'd like. After the heavy storms last month, the storm drain there backs up badly and the water spreads clear across the crosswalk. It sat for the better part of two days. Yesterday I nearly slipped stepping off the curb into it. I've written before about this corner, and I hate to keep asking, but with more rain in the forecast I'm genuinely worried. Would you look into it personally? I'd be so grateful to have it cleared before the next downpour.
